workflow_name: Customer Inquiry Thread -> Response + Pipeline Note
user_message: I have this escalation email from Wide World Importers that needs draft the follow-up. Need this before my 2pm call.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Ryan Kowalski <ryan.kowalski@wideworldimp.com>
To: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-02 15:22 EST
Subject: URGENT: partner engagement Issue at Wide World Importers

There's been a change in our timeline for partner engagement. Can we discuss the implications on the overall project plan?

---
From: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
To: Ryan Kowalski <ryan.kowalski@wideworldimp.com>
Date: 2024-02-06 15:10 EST
Subject: Follow-up: partner engagement Review

There's been a change in our timeline for partner engagement. Can we discuss the implications on the overall project plan?

---
From: Ryan Kowalski <ryan.kowalski@wideworldimp.com>
To: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-20 14:09 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Wide World Importers partner engagement

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about partner engagement. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

signals.detail: Signal detected for Wide World Importers: there's a dependency on the security team's review that could delay us

handoffs.condition: MSX update warranted from thread content

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF33-N trap (Draft-only default delays response on simple acknowledgments); produces complete Thread analysis, draft response, waiting-on-whom assessment, MSX pipeline note proposal (if warrante
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF33-N failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF33-N failure mode (Draft-only default delays response on simple acknowledgments)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct partner engagement decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF33-N -- Draft-only default delays response on simple acknowledgments
Guardrail axis scored neutrally -- focus on whether output served the user's actual need
